name: Maria Schneider
abstract: Maria Lynn Schneider (born November 27, 1960) is an American composer and jazz orchestra leader who has won multiple Grammy Awards.

interviews.title: Maria Schneider interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

concerts.label: Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: Women in Jazz
Spring for Music: Saint Paul Chamber Orchestra
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Josephine Davies
about_quotes.evidence: I heard this beautiful thing yesterday which was something about we have so little knowledge really about what consciousness is... [Interviewer mentions conversation with Maria Schneider] ... [Interviewee] Yup. Absolutely. Yeah. 100 percent I support that statement.
about_quotes.doc_title: Josephine Davies interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 14, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: David Friesen
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] You know Maria Schneider said something along those lines. She encouraged her students to not be so one-sided about just music, like live your life. And you have to be a full person in order to create something meaningful.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Friesen interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 24, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Jonathan Scales
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Maria Schneider said something that I just found was very illuminating... [INTERVIEWEE] Yes. I actually had this conversation with someone last night... I was trying to say the same thing to them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jonathan Scales interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 23, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Christine Jensen
about_quotes.evidence: I totally agree with her. I mean I’m told to have this app on my phone that’s called “Spotify Artist” where I count, and the industry is counting how many people are listening to my music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Christine Jensen interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 4, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: a piano player from a big band with Maria Schneider. I wanted to have her on the show and she says, “oh I play terrible piano.” She absolutely refused to do it, to do the show
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, April 26, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: John LaBarbera
about_quotes.evidence: I think Maria Schneider’s last CD was a finger point at all of the digital lords out there... But she’s pointing fingers at these large corporations
